column1: # persisted-paths.tsv - single source of truth for personal-features' persisted
# bind-mount paths. Adding, removing, or renaming a persisted path means editing
# THIS file; every consumer below derives from it, and CI fails if any of them
# drifts out of sync.
# Consumers:
#   install.sh               creates each container_target (mkdir -p / chown / chmod mode)
#                            EXCEPT host-provisioned rows (provision=host): those the
#                            container must NOT create - see the provision column below
#   setup.sh                 creates each host_source under the host home directory
#                            (mkdir -p / chmod mode - the container sees the HOST
#                            mode through an active bind mount, so the mode column
#                            must be enforced on both sides), and initializes the
#                            host-provisioned tracker database schema
#   setup.ps1                mirrors setup.sh row-for-row (hand-kept in sync; no pwsh CI
#                            for the container build, so parity is checked statically by
#                            .github/scripts/test_host_setup_parity.py)
#   check_persisted_paths.py asserts devcontainer-feature.json's mounts + containerEnv
#                            match these rows. The JSON cannot be generated from this
#                            file: release-please patches $.version into the checked-in
#                            JSON in place, so it must stay hand-authored and be checked.
# Tab-separated. Columns:
#   name             stable identifier for the path (docs / error messages only)
#   host_source      bind-mount source, relative to the host home dir ($HOME / %USERPROFILE%)
#   container_target bind-mount target (absolute path inside the container)
#   env_var          containerEnv variable that points at the target, or - if none
#   env_value        exact value of env_var in the JSON, or - when env_var is -.
#                    Usually identical to container_target; the mempalace row is
#                    the exception - MEMPALACE_PALACE_PATH names the `palace`
#                    subdirectory INSIDE the mount, because mempalace keeps the
#                    palace under a directory it may also put other state in.
#   mode             octal permissions applied to the path: install.sh chmods
#                    container_target, setup.sh chmods host_source (setup.ps1
#                    does not - NTFS has no POSIX modes, and Docker Desktop
#                    synthesizes in-container bits for shared paths). 0700 for
#                    the credential-holding dirs (#233) and the billing DB (#369);
#                    0777 for shell-history so any container uid can create/append
#                    bash_history through the bind mount even when it does not own
#                    the host dir (#323); 0755 otherwise.
#   provision        who creates the container_target: `container` = install.sh
#                    mkdir/chown/chmods it at build time (the common case);
#                    `host` = the host provisions it (setup.sh/setup.ps1) and it is
#                    ONLY ever a bind mount, so install.sh must NOT pre-create it
#                    (#369). Pre-creating a host-provisioned target would make a
#                    missing/misconfigured mount indistinguishable from a working
#                    one - the container would find an empty dir and silently build
#                    a fresh, container-local, rebuild-discarded database.
# A trailing slash marks a DIRECTORY (created with mkdir -p); no trailing slash
# marks a FILE (created with touch, after mkdir -p of its parent). Docker
# materialises a *missing* single-file mount source as a directory, which then
# fails the mount, so file sources must be touched into existence - hence the
# distinction. All eight paths are directories today.
# name
claude
gh
odoo_sdk
pr-automation
coderabbit
shell-history
odoo-tracker
mempalace
